outlook.headline: Areal density gains are broadening across the HDD chain
outlook.summary: Higher bit density is now a clear near-term positive for both suppliers and drive makers: Entegris sees more materials consumption, while Seagate and Western Digital cite better economics, TCO, and customer adoption. The mention trend is also firming from a low base, suggesting this remains an increasingly important talking point into the next 1-2 quarters. Watch whether continued density progress translates into sustained pricing power and tighter supply discipline.

drivers.label: Higher bit density lifts materials intensity
drivers.detail: More advanced areal density drives greater materials consumption and is explicitly called favorable for the supply chain.

drivers.label: Areal density improves drive economics
drivers.detail: Advancing density supports profit economics alongside supply discipline and pricing.

drivers.label: Better TCO boosts adoption confidence
drivers.detail: Higher areal density improves customer total cost of ownership and supports drive adoption.

drivers.label: Mention trend is strengthening
drivers.detail: Company mentions rose to 0.3x with 21% momentum vs trailing average, signaling building focus rather than fading interest.

risks: If areal density progress slows, the economics and adoption benefits could fade quickly.
Any loosening of supply discipline would dilute pricing support for Seagate and Western Digital.
Customer adoption could lag if TCO gains do not translate into enough real-world savings versus alternatives.
